title: centaurea cariensiformis
intro: Centaurea cariensiformis, also known as Kermes oak or Mountain cornflower, is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Centaurea cariensiformis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a popular remedy in herbal medicine. Traditionally, the plant's flowers, leaves, and roots are used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties.
